source_fact_summary: The public issue says functional-tests began failing on main immediately after PR #2947 merged, with all four triage eval cases failing because URL-sourced scripts were not in allowed_remote_resources. The linked PR reports go vet was clean, targeted Go tests passed, and the PR was generated with Claude Code.
unsupported_claims: The exact boundary between human authorship and Claude Code generation for PR #2947 still requires manual confirmation from the PR metadata and body.
category_change_reason: No category change suggested: the paired issue and PR show static/unit checks passing before a real functional runtime path failed.
privacy_findings: Only public GitHub issue or PR links and short factual summaries are retained; no credentials, private files, raw logs, or local absolute paths were copied.
severity_reason: The merge broke functional triage runs across all cited eval cases despite passing targeted build/static checks.
missing_information: Manual review should verify the exact PR test-plan text, Actions logs, and minimal functional reproduction path.
scope_reason: This is a target-project delivery where the PR's Go vet and targeted tests passed but the real functional harness path failed after merge. Build logs plus functional-test logs can independently validate the mismatch.
notes: Codex review suggestion only: public-source accessibility and category/scope recommendations do not mark the candidate source_verified, approved, or reproduced. Manual confirmation remains required.
